LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO MARCH-14-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF GLEB FINKELMAN'S MOTION OF GLEB FINKELMAN TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION FROM DEFENDANT CLEAN ROOFING; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF MOVES TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES, AND FOR SANCTIONS, FROM DEFENDANT CLEAN ROOFING. PLAINTIFF'S MOVING PAPERS DEMONSTRATED THAT CLEAN ROOFING DID NOT PROVIDE CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, ALTHOUGH IT DID PRODUCE DOCUMENTS (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO MARCH-14-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF GLEB FINKELMAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION FROM DEFENDANT CLEAN SOLAR; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF MOVES TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES, AND FOR SANCTIONS, FROM DEFENDANT CLEAN SOLAR, INC. PLAINTIFF'S MOVING PAPERS DEMONSTRATED THAT CLEAN SOLAR DID NOT PROVIDE CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, ALTHOUGH IT DID PRODUCE DOCUMENTS (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production From Defendant Clean Solar
Off Calendar Mar-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. PAM SCHOLEFIELD, Esq. of Scholefield P.C. - Construction Law (tel: 858-613-0888) for Plaintiff.
Atty. RYAN MCHUGH, Esq. of The Angell Law Firm, P.C. (tel: 925-947-2525) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF GLEB FINKELMAN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production From Defendant Clean Solar.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff moves to compel further responses, and for sanctions, from defendant Clean Solar, Inc. Plaintiff's moving papers demonstrated that Clean Solar did not provide code-compliant responses to Plaintiff's Requests for Production of Documents, Set One, although it did produce documents. In particular, Clean Solar's responses did not contain a representation of compliance (CCP 2031.210(a)(1)) or a statement of objection and the documents falling within a category to which objection was made (CCP 2031.240(b)(1)). Clean Solar now contends the motion is moot because it provided code-compliant responses after Plaintiff filed his motion. (McHugh Dec. para. 6 & Ex. A.) The court finds Clean Solar has now provided compliant responses but its failure to do so in meet and confer (and its failure to extend Plaintiff's time to file a motion to compel) necessitated this motion. The court does not find that Clean Solar acted with substantial justification and awards sanctions of $750 payable to Plaintiff by Clean Solar within 10 court days of entry of this order. (See Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 390, 405 [sanctions provision applies even where party serves responses after motion is filed].) The discovery statutes are supposed to be self-executing and a party should not be required to bring a motion to obtain a compliant response.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF GLEB FINKELMAN's Motion Of Gleb Finkelman To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production From Defendant Clean Roofing.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff moves to compel further responses, and for sanctions, from defendant Clean Roofing. Plaintiff's moving papers demonstrated that Clean Roofing did not provide code-compliant responses to Plaintiff's Requests for Production of Documents, Set One, although it did produce documents. In particular, Clean Roofing's responses did not contain a representation of compliance (CCP 2031.210(a)(1)) or a statement of objection and the documents falling within a category to which objection was made (CCP 2031.240(b)(1)). Clean Roofing now contends the motion is moot because it provided code-compliant responses after Plaintiff filed his motion. (McHugh Dec. para. 6 & Ex. B.) The court finds Clean Roofing has now provided compliant responses but its failure to do so in meet and confer (and its failure to extend Plaintiff's time to file a motion to compel) necessitated this motion. The court does not find that Clean Roofing acted with substantial justification and awards sanctions of $750 payable to Plaintiff by Clean Solar within 10 court days of entry of this order. (See Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 390, 405 [sanctions provision applies even where party serves responses after motion is filed].) The discovery statutes are supposed to be self-executing and a party should not be required to bring a motion to obtain a compliant response.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
